notes: NI Session Guitarist series — Picked Nylon, Electric Mint, Strummed Acoustic, Strummed Acoustic 2. All variants share the same Quick Control layout, though exact labels differ slightly by instrument. CC 64 (Sustain) holds strummed chords; release to choke. CC 11 (Expression) controls overall level/dynamics. Key switches or chord keys trigger specific chord voicings or patterns depending on mode. Right-click any knob in Kontakt → Learn MIDI CC to verify assignments for your specific variant.

Expression.description: Expression — dynamics and output level. Use for fade-ins and natural playing volume variation.

Modulation.description: Strum speed or performance variation — instrument-dependent

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds chord in strummed modes; release to choke/mute

Quick Control 1.description: Pick / Strum Position — bridge (bright/cutting) vs neck (warm/mellow)

Quick Control 2.description: Tone — overall tonal brightness; high = sparkly, low = warm body

Quick Control 4.description: Delay Amount — built-in stereo delay mix

Quick Control 5.description: Chorus / Modulation Depth — add shimmer or movement to the tone

Quick Control 6.description: Attack Variation — randomise pick attack timing (realism)

Quick Control 7.description: String Noise — fret buzz and slide noise level (higher = rawer feel)

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and timing variation between notes
